title: Roasted Norwegian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Norwegian lamb stew featuring lamb shoulder, roasted to perfection and served in a fondue pot. This dish is perfect for special occasions and showcases the rich flavors of Norway.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb pieces with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb pieces until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, thyme, and rosemary to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the lamb broth and red wine to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Transfer the stew to a fondue pot and serve hot.
